APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:

Michelle Golden of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing The Current Trial Date with Preference; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). The Court did not consider the conditional non-opposition by Defendant Morse TEC LLC, F/K/A BorgWarner Morse TEC LLC for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.) As such, the Court also did not consider the joinder by ZF North America, Inc.

The trial date of May 1, 2023 is vacated. The new trial date is March 6, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is March 17, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 2, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 17, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 3, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 16, 2022. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

No later than December 1, 2022, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than December 1, 2022, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for January 12, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 20, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 3, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 24, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, March 2, 2023 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
